metadata.title: Irreversible Action Gate: Before, Not After
metadata.description_blurb: Agents do not distinguish reversible from irreversible operations — the gate belongs to the human, placed before execution, because once sent it is sent, and only before catches the error.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: She gave the agent a task: send a follow-up email to all contacts who had not responded to the program invitation. She did not specify which contacts. The agent sent 847 emails before she read the first completion message. Some were to contacts who had responded via a channel the agent did not check. Some were to contacts who had explicitly declined. She had not placed a gate before the send action. There is no gate after. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, irreversible action gate instruction

props.command: Any action that sends, submits, or deletes — stop and confirm with me before proceeding.

beats.narration_text: Agents do not distinguish reversible from irreversible operations. The model uses available tools to complete the task description. Reversibility is a human judgment — it depends on downstream consequences that the agent cannot fully model. Moving a file to a temp folder is reversible. Permanently deleting it is not. Drafting an email is reversible. Sending it is not. The agent treats both as task steps unless the human places a gate.
beats.new_visual_element: No auto-distinction — agent uses available tools / reversibility is human judgment / draft vs. send / delete vs. temp

beats.narration_text: Irreversible actions share three properties: they cross a system boundary, they cannot be undone with a file-system undo, and they touch systems you do not fully control. Send covers email, messaging, form submission, API calls with side effects. Submit covers any handoff to an external system. Delete covers file deletion, record removal, publication reversal. Any action that would be catastrophic if wrong on the first run belongs behind a gate.
beats.new_visual_element: Recognition sign — crosses system boundary / cannot undo / external system / send / submit / delete
beats.spark_line: Crosses boundary. Cannot undo. Gate it.

beats.narration_text: Gate placement is everything. A gate placed before the action is supervision. A gate placed after — reviewing what was done — is an audit log. Only before catches the error. The design rule: any action you would not want executed by accident requires human initiation, not human approval after. The agent lists proposed actions, waits, you read and approve, then execution begins. This is not a workaround. This is the correct architecture.
beats.new_visual_element: Gate placement — before = supervision / after = audit / human initiation not approval after / correct architecture
beats.spark_line: Before is supervision. After is audit.

beats.narration_text: The irreversible action gate is one clause in the task brief: any action that sends, submits, or deletes — stop and confirm before proceeding. It does not slow the agent on reversible steps. It stops the agent at the exact moment when the stakes change from recoverable to permanent. Not every agentic task needs every element — but every agentic task that touches external systems needs this one.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — task brief with irreversible gate clause / stop at send/submit/delete / recoverable vs. permanent
beats.spark_line: One clause. Stops at the right moment.

beats.narration_text: Add this gate clause to your next agentic task brief. Paste this.

props.command: Add this gate to your task brief before any agentic run that touches external systems: 'Before any action that sends, submits, or deletes — including email, form submission, file deletion, or any API call with side effects — stop completely. List what you intend to do, who or what it affects, and whether it can be undone. Wait for my explicit approval before proceeding. Do not send, submit, or delete without confirmation.'

beats.narration_text: If this stops the send before 847 emails go out, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
